title: Pseudobaccharis nervosa
taxonomy: Pseudobaccharis nervosa is a plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. This classification places it within the larger clade of flowering plants (angiosperms) and more specifically within the clade of eudicots. The family Asteraceae, also known as the daisy or sunflower family, comprises a wide range of flowering plants, including many species of daisies, sunflowers, and asters. Pseudobaccharis nervosa is a member of the tribe Mutisieae, which includes many plant species native to the Americas. The classification of Pseudobaccharis nervosa reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the aster family.

intro: Pseudobaccharis nervosa, also known as nervosa baccharis or nervosa, is a plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae), native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering relief and promoting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Pseudobaccharis nervosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are popular preparations used to harness its health benefits.
